starting_state.K: Reader hears a polished update without a real observation.
starting_state.P: Reader gets generic iteration language rather than a next priority.

target_state.K: Reader sees the core funnel problem clearly.
target_state.P: Reader knows the next move is pricing and onboarding clarity.

displacement.K: Shift from vague progress language to one observed issue.
displacement.P: Shift from generic iteration to a named next priority.
displacement.A: Shift from passive update-reading to shared focus.

layers.assert: Acknowledge that the week did include customer conversations and signal.
layers.exception: Break the polished surface by naming the real funnel tension.
layers.handler: Install the new frame around pricing confusion and onboarding.
layers.log: Leave the team with a clear next move.

modules.name: old-belief-index
modules.output_state: Reader sees the lack of observation
modules.interface_hook: The useful update starts where the generic one breaks.

think_aloud_frames: "Sounds like a fine week, but I still do not know what changed."
"This could describe almost any startup update."
"The team learned something, but I cannot tell what matters now."

modules.name: expectation-break
modules.interface_hook: That shifts the team's next priority.

cta.critical_incident: Customer conversations show the product is landing but the pricing story is blocking self-serve conviction.
cta.absurd_old_conclusion: If generic excitement language were enough, pricing confusion would not still be leaking momentum.
cta.switch_parameter: The decisive parameter is funnel friction, not morale wording.
cta.collapse_signal: The polished update stops working once the real pricing issue is named.

modules.name: new-framework
logic: Reframe the update around the funnel signal.
modules.interface_hook: Now the decision is what to do next.

cta.goal: Turn a vague progress update into a decision-bearing founder note.
methods: Replace broad optimism with one tension.
Use customer signal to justify focus.
End with a clear next step.
selection_rules: If the update only says the week was productive, cut it.
If the update identifies the bottleneck and next move, keep it.

modules.name: boundary-and-timing-anchor
logic: State the next focus area explicitly.
modules.interface_hook: That makes the update directional instead of decorative.

timeline.now: Name the pricing and onboarding problem.
timeline.soon: Test clearer pricing and self-serve messaging.
timeline.later: Judge whether conversion improves or the hypothesis weakens.

cta.checklist_template: Over the next ____ weeks we will test ____ in onboarding. If conversion does not improve, we will revisit ____.
cta.downgrade_condition: If the next update still avoids a concrete funnel tension, downgrade confidence in the team's diagnostic clarity.

modules.name: identity-embed
logic: End on an owned operating move.
modules.output_state: Team can retell the update as a decision

rebuttal_lines: "A good founder update does not just sound positive; it names the bottleneck."
"Customer signal matters only when it changes the next priority."
"If the note cannot tell us what to fix next, it is not really an update."
retell_bullets: A useful update ends with a decision.
cta.identity_lock_sentence: Although polished progress language feels safer, I choose to write updates around the real bottleneck and the next move.

spec.objective: Make the update sound observed, specific, and decisive.

diagnose.main_issue: The update gestures at learning but never names the actual problem discovered.

rewrite.central_claim: The product is landing, but pricing confusion is slowing self-serve conversion and should be the next priority.
edits: Replace broad optimism with one concrete observation from customer conversations.
Make the pricing and onboarding issue explicit.
End with the next move instead of a generic commitment to iterate.

remaining_risks: Evidence is still compact because the format is short.

notes: The update names the real issue.
The tone moves from polish to ownership.
The sequence stays coherent from observation to next move.
